name: Harvest Circle
identity: Cultural and market town where harvest patron-families, quota clerks, and festival brokers turn field output into alliance redistribution.
approach: Open market rings, wagon circles, drying racks, and shrine lawns announce a town built for exchange as much as storage.
inside: Granaries, counting courts, communal tables, festival grounds, and family compounds share one busy civic center.
who_is_out_of_place: people who insult local rites or cut market lines
expected_behavior: respect family-controlled stalls and convoy order
treat festival exchange as civic duty as well as profit
faith: unity and harvest rites are public, crowded, and used to reset debts, favors, and work pledges
seasonal_change: the town swells dramatically at harvest and tightens into reserve-counting discipline when shortages threaten.
law_context: market peace is enforced through communal law, clerk records, and family responsibility for stall, storage, and convoy conduct

sensory_surface.detail: Open market rings, wagon circles, drying racks, and shrine lawns announce a town built for exchange as much as storage.
Granaries, counting courts, communal tables, festival grounds, and family compounds share one busy civic center.

social_expectation_surface.detail: respect family-controlled stalls and convoy order
treat festival exchange as civic duty as well as profit

public_behavior_texture: People who belong here are usually farmers, carters, grain factors, and public conduct is read through expectations such as declare loads honestly, respect family-controlled stalls and convoy order.
visible_power_signals: Ordinary decisions are visibly shaped by harvest stewards, quota clerks, patron-families.
local_tension_surface: The place feels stable only until pressures such as spoilage, price pressure, favor feuds begin showing through daily routines.
differentiator: Harvest Circle stands apart because its economy leans on grain redistribution, festival trade; public faith remains visible through unity and harvest rites are public, crowded, and used to reset debts, favors, and work pledges; outsiders are noticed fastest when they resemble cheats, panic buyers.
encounter_rhythm: Most first scenes in Harvest Circle begin through market harvest town rhythms: arrival, observation, minor correction, and only then deeper access.
scene_openers: A first arrival at Harvest Circle immediately highlights cultural and market town where harvest patron-families, quota clerks, and festival brokers turn field output into alliance redistribution
The quickest social read of Harvest Circle comes from how locals react to declare loads honestly / respect family-controlled stalls and convoy order.
social_misstep_examples: Treating Harvest Circle like a casual stop rather than a governed space.
Ignoring local expectation to declare loads honestly; respect family-controlled stalls and convoy order.
low_grade_conflict_triggers: A queue, toll, or inspection dispute tied to grain redistribution.
A local intermediary resents outsider assumptions about Harvest Circle.
institutional_escalation_path: Workshop or neighborhood correction in Harvest Circle
Escalation into law, tariff, or faction attention if ignored
outsider_advantage: Outsiders can sometimes notice pressure fractures in Harvest Circle before residents admit them.
outsider_liability: Outsiders in Harvest Circle are judged quickly by whether they understand its governing rhythms.
public_complication_table: A minor official wants paperwork clarified.
A public argument exposes pressure around spoilage.
Someone mistakes the party for agents tied to harvest stewards.
A delay at a gate, quay, shrine, or market creates heat.
A small errand reveals a larger local imbalance.
private_pressure_table: A household or guild contact is quietly overextended.
A local fixer asks for help with a contained problem.
A hidden cost behind grain redistribution surfaces.
A trusted intermediary is being watched.
A faith, law, or trade obligation has slipped.
A personal favor could become a faction debt.

arrival_variants_by_time.dawn: At dawn, Harvest Circle feels procedural and watchful as daily rhythms set.
arrival_variants_by_time.midday: By midday, Harvest Circle is busiest and least forgiving of confusion.
arrival_variants_by_time.night: At night, Harvest Circle reveals who really moves after formal structures thin out.

recent_local_change_signal: Locals in Harvest Circle mention that something small but important has changed in the last few weeks, even if they refuse to name it directly.
